question.text: An investor buys 500 shares of ABC Ltd at Rs. 200 per share on Monday. The settlement cycle is T+1. By when must the investor make payment?

answer.text: Tuesday (the next business day after the trade)
Wednesday (two business days after the trade)
Within one week of the trade

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under T+1 settlement, trades executed on day T must be settled the next business day (T+1). If the investor buys on Monday (T), payment must be made by Tuesday (T+1). The total payment = 500 x Rs. 200 = Rs. 1,00,000. **The Trap:** Same day (option b) would be T+0 settlement, which is optional and not the standard cycle. Wednesday (option c) would be T+2, the old settlement cycle that has been replaced. **Cross-Exam Context:** In practice, brokers may debit the purchase amount from the linked bank account on the trade day itself, but the formal settlement with the clearing corporation is on T+1. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** T+1 = Next working day. If trade on Monday, settle Tuesday. If trade on Friday, settle Monday (next business day, skipping weekend). Simple but frequently tested.
